analysis.name: Cassie Williams

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Williams is one of my favorite math professors ever. She made getting up for a 7:45 AM lecture--dare I say it--enjoyable?! Her style of teaching made it very clear what was going to be on each exam.

Dr Williams made Calc 3 interesting for me. Her explanations of complex topics are clear and concise. She even posted annotated guided notes along with the corresponding lecture video for anyone who missed class. Tests are fair, but are weighted heavily (87%). As long as you do past exams for practice, you should do well. 10/10 would take again.

I loved her! She knows what she's teaching and is thorough in her explanations. There's a lot of time spent on problem examples in class. The class is test heavy but she explains everything well and the lecture videos/notes are always posted. My TA was also great, and the discussions (even tho not mandatory) are helpful.

She has a great personality and does a good job of explaining concepts. However, WAY too much time is spent doing activities during lecture, to the point that we are constantly behind on material and have to rush through some topics. There was a lecture where she gave us nearly 20 min to do a line integral that most ppl finished in less than 5 min.

Dr Williams was an amazing professor for Calc 3 - she was very clear in her explanations and made the content easy to digest. She gave note templates online to use for lecture which is very helpful because it made the lecture easier to follow along with. She definitely made going to her 7:45 am lecture worth it!

I took this professor because I enjoy failing my classes. I love destroying my mental health. Green single handedly got my parents divorced and made me drop out of college. Chelsey is a good enough professor but you will need to spend some time on the homeworks to make sure you fully understand them for the test.

take his class u won't regret it! His class is very low stress and he does a really good job at making the material interesting!

The competition can be tight (lots of people do very well) so you really have to go above and beyond, but good grades are certainly attainable. Exams were fair and only tested material that was covered in class. Project lacked guidance and was very open ended with unclear evaluation criteria.

Professor Linderoth is an interesting character. You can tell he has a lot of background in Optimization and has worked with some pretty big names in the field. Professor Linderoth is amazing. This class is extremely difficult, but gives you everything you need to learn. (LIS 646 NOT 440)

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed his class. It was pretty easy and mostly writing assignments. We do lots of peer reviews, and he gives thorough feedback quickly. He grades generously. Participation is mandatory, but I only spoke a few times and still got an A. Just show up and do the work. He is a nice guy and truly cares about his students.

Pretty good professor for a pretty boring course, generous with grades, gives good feedback, generally cares for his students and is a pretty nice guy overall. He is Colombian though so his accent and arrangement of words are a bit weird sometimes (his vocabulary and writing skills are great though!)

Overall, the class was a fun time. The Professor was a blast to have and always in a good mood. I would recommend participating in class when he asks questions or thoughts to get a good grade for attendance and participation. He does have a little bit of broken English, but can still understand clearly.

Oscar is a really funny and interactive professor! I participated in class discussions maybe 7 times and got an A for participation. He is very good at teaching the course content. It is an English class, so get ready to write a lot. He gives very good feedback on writing assignments.

Óscar Useche is a really personable professor. He does a thorough job explaining course content to make sure we understand the content. He also has scheduled meetings to make sure you are understanding the content. He provides helpful feedback on work and is a flexible grader. Would want him as a professor again!

review_highlights.comment: Online canvas modules with weekly podcasts and a synchronous discussion. Lots of reading and weekly discussion posts. The homework is at times overwhelming with the essays. Sometimes it felt like he was more focused on themes of gender roles than the actual events of the WW1, but I did learn a lot about the societal background of the time.

History 350 is an online course with Usisshkin and was great overall. There is a lot of assignments and discussions because it is an online course so you have to stay on top of your work. The tests and papers have very clear grading and it's not hard to get an A if you try. He doesn't make WWI boring and is a great overall online class!
Pretty good professor! Lectures can be a bit unclear at times, but the slides + office hours are really helpful!

Professor Ussishkin was great. In a small seminar environment, he did a really good job of facilitating a super engaging discussion and you could tell he really cares about what he teaches. He's definitely not the clearest professor when it comes to putting stuff on canvas, but he's a super easy grader so if you put the effort in, you'll get an A.

I would absolutely take this class again, but it was not my favorite. Ussishkin isn't the best lecturer, but he's very knowledgeable and passionate about the subject and his students. I would say if you don't enjoy history to some extent, this class isn't for you. The class was basically pass/fail, so if you put effort in, you will get an A.

analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently

review_highlights.comment: Course is designed down to a science. Some readings are mandatory, others are not. Quizzes become increasingly difficult. Problem sets are consistently difficult. Exams are generally difficult. Generous grading for all assignments. One can reference an enormous catalogue of past content for additional help. The course is extremely rewarding.

He is organized and clear on what's expected of you. He always hands out the answers to the homework right after you turn it in, and the homework is always returned the next class. Textbook is not needed. He uploads practice problem sets and also every past exam since 2007 online. Be wary, the material in the third part of the course is very hard.

Logic can be dry but Prof. Vranas explains it very clearly. If you can manage to pay attention you should be well prepared to do well in the class. You will need to practice outside of class if you intend to do well. Also beware, this isn't a traditional philosophy course... it's more akin to math.

Logic changed me.
The most well-prepared class ever. Peter gives clear lectures throughout the semester and there's little confusion about content if you pay active attention to class
The last 2 weeks' content is challenging, so work on more problems. A very uncommon question appears on the final, but if you do RECENT past exams you should be good.

An OG if there ever was one. But seriously, great experience taking his logic class - amazing TA Jordan and Peter organized everything so well. The material itself is quite useful, the grading scale lenient, and the structure of the course is exceedingly logical - 'nuff said.
